text:
PRATICAL NO 21
1. How do you configure Selenium IDE for a specific base URL?
•
Step 1: Install Selenium IDE as a browser extension from Chrome Web Store or Firefox
Add-ons.
•
Step 2: Open Selenium IDE by clicking on the extension icon in the browser.
•
Step 3: Click on 'Create a new project'.
•
Step 4: Enter the Base URL (for example: https://www.example.com).
•
Step 5: Give a project name and click 'Create'.
•
Step 6: The Base URL will now be set. All relative paths used in test cases will be appended to
this Base URL.
•
Step 7: You can modify the Base URL anytime from Project Settings if required.
2. How do you record a test case in Selenium IDE?
•
Step 1: Open Selenium IDE in the browser.
•
Step 2: Open an existing project or create a new project with a Base URL.
•
Step 3: Click on 'Record' button.
•
Step 4: Navigate through the website and perform actions (clicks, typing, form submission,
etc.).
•
Step 5: Selenium IDE automatically records all user interactions as commands.
•
Step 6: Click on 'Stop Recording' once the test steps are completed.
•
Step 7: Save the test case with an appropriate name.
•
Step 8: Click on 'Run' button to execute the recorded test case.
3. Enlist factors considered for selecting a testing tool for test
automation.
•
Application Type: Web, Mobile, Desktop, or API testing support.
•
Compatibility: Support for required browsers, operating systems, and devices.
•
Programming Language Support: Availability of preferred language like Java, Python, etc.
•
Ease of Use: User-friendly interface and easy learning curve.
•
Integration Capability: Support for CI/CD tools like Jenkins and version control systems.
•
Reporting and Logging: Detailed test reports and error logs.
•
Cost: Open-source or licensed tool based on budget.
•
Community Support: Availability of documentation, tutorials, and active user community.
•
Scalability: Ability to handle large and complex test suites.
•
Maintenance: Easy test script maintenance and updates.
